description: Multiple cash deposits below the $10,000 Currency Transaction Report (CTR) threshold detected over a 10-day window. Eight (8) cash deposits ranging from $9,100 to $9,950 were made across three branch locations between November 4-14, 2024, totaling $76,450. This pattern is consistent with structuring under 31 CFR § 1010.100(xx) — the intentional splitting of currency transactions to evade CTR reporting requirements.

Legal entity customer Meridian Capital Holdings LLC (CUST-002) has initiated 15 international wire transfers totaling $1,875,000 over the past 90 days to counterparties in high-risk jurisdictions including the Russian Federation, Cyprus (offshore financial center), and Panama. The beneficial owner Dmitri Testovsky is a former Russian government official (PEP). Wire activity significantly exceeds the customer's stated expected activity profile ($500,000/month). Multiple wire destinations include entities with generic 'Holdings' and 'Management' names consistent with shell company use.

Individual account (Jennifer L. Testaccount, CUST-003) that was dormant for approximately 6 months (last meaningful activity May 2024) suddenly received $450,000 in inbound wire transfers over a 72-hour window (November 17-19, 2024), followed immediately by near-complete disbursement via multiple outbound international wires to Singapore, Panama, and Hong Kong. The account had no international wire capability at the time of account opening in 2019; this capability was added without relationship manager review in May 2024, immediately prior to the resumption of activity. The ML model detected this pattern with 94% confidence as consistent with third-party money laundering or account compromise/takeover.
